title: Global Variable Library
text: Global Variable Library Use this editor to define global variables that are available to all applications that use the library in which it is defined. Note: It is usually preferable to use component and local variables, rather than global variables. Global variables are accessible to all ProcScript in an application's components, and to global ProcScripts that use the same library or the system library. In a large complex application, the risk of several components overwriting the value of global variables increases. When using these variables it is important to clear them when they are no longer required. Any component that uses a global variable must specify the library in which it is located. Failure to do so will generate a ProcScript compiler error. Global variables of type Handle cannot be used for accessing operations. Instead, use a component handle on a service. For more information, see Handles . Properties Variable Name Description Data Type Classification Display Format (Global Variable) Comments How to Working with Global Variables Menu Options Table 1. Menu Options for Message Libraries Menu Option Action File > Open/New Open or create a new library. File > Compile File > Compile all File > Compile modified Compile the current, all, or modified global objects. Edit > New <Global Object Type> Create a new global object Edit > Duplicate <Global Object Type> Save a copy of the current global object under a different name. Edit > Delete <Global Object Type> Delete the current global object. View >Zoom Field Edit the selected field in a separate dialog; useful when editing text blocks and code. View >Message Frame Display the message frame; useful after compiling the object. Go To <Global Object Type> Create or open a different type of global object, or library (properties). Related Links Variables Variables are placeholders for data whose value can change during the runtime duration of the ProcScript module, component, or application for which they have scope. The scope is determined by where they are declared. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
